seoTitle: How to Upload a Canva Video to YouTube
metaDescription: Learn how to export a Canva video and upload it to YouTube, with tips for optimizing quality and settings before publishing.
intro: Once a video is finished in Canva, getting it onto YouTube just takes a proper export and a few upload settings. This guide covers exporting your Canva project and publishing it to YouTube smoothly.

steps.heading: Finish editing your Canva video
steps.body: Make sure all clips, text, and audio are finalized before exporting.

steps.heading: Export the video from Canva
steps.body: Click Share, then Download, and choose the MP4 video format at your preferred quality setting.

steps.heading: Sign in to YouTube Studio
steps.body: Go to YouTube and open Studio, then click Create > Upload Video.

steps.heading: Upload your exported file
steps.body: Select the MP4 file you downloaded from Canva and let it upload.

steps.heading: Add title, description, and thumbnail
steps.body: Fill in your video's metadata and publishing settings before making it public.

faq.question: What video quality should I export from Canva for YouTube?
faq.answer: Export at the highest resolution your project supports, ideally 1080p or higher, for the best YouTube playback quality.

faq.question: Does Canva have a direct 'publish to YouTube' option?
faq.answer: Some Canva plans include direct social sharing integrations; otherwise, download the file and upload manually through YouTube Studio.

faq.question: How long does it take to export a video from Canva?
faq.answer: Export time depends on video length and complexity, typically a few minutes for standard clips.

faq.question: Can I re-upload an updated version of my video later?
faq.answer: Yes, but YouTube treats it as a new upload — you'd need to edit your existing video's metadata separately if you want to keep the same URL.
